page.title: Marbella Flamenco Festival 2026: Yacht Charter Guide

data.meta_description: Book a marbella flamenco festival yacht charter for 2026. Watch flamenco from the sea on a private boat from Puerto Banús. Prices from €749/2h.
data.summary: Plan your Marbella Flamenco Festival 2026 yacht charter with our guide. Depart from Puerto Banús on a private boat for prime views of the festival's waterfront performances.
key_facts: Marbella Flamenco Festival 2026 dates typically in July (check local listings).
Charter a boat from Puerto Banús for front-row sea views of flamenco shows.
Our fleet includes 18 boats, from 6.4m speedboats to 29m superyachts.
Prices start at €749/2h for a Tier A boat (Astondoa 40 or Azimut 39).
All charters include skipper, fuel, drinks, snacks, insurance, and IVA.
Mangusta 80 charters (from €4,719/4h) include a free jet ski.
Private charter means flexible timing—arrive before crowds, leave when you like.
Nearby anchor spots: Cala del Faro, Cala Cortés, and Cabopino.

faq.q: When is the Marbella Flamenco Festival 2026?
faq.a: The Marbella Flamenco Festival typically takes place in July, with exact dates announced a few months prior. Check the official Marbella tourism website for 2026 dates. We recommend booking your yacht charter early, as boats fill up fast during festival week.

faq.q: Can I watch flamenco shows from a boat?
faq.a: Yes. Some festival performances take place along the Marbella coastline or at venues near the water, such as the Puerto Banús marina. A private boat lets you anchor close to the action, giving you a unique sea-level perspective without the crowds on land.

faq.q: Which boat is best for a group of 10?
faq.a: For a group of 10, the Azimut 39 (11 pax) or the Fairline Targa 12m (10 pax) are excellent choices. Both depart from Puerto Banús and include a skipper, drinks, and snacks. Pricing for these boats starts at €749/2h.

faq.q: What is included in the charter price?
faq.a: Every charter includes a licensed skipper, fuel, water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava, light snacks, insurance, safety equipment, and Spanish IVA (21%). There are no hidden fees—just show up and enjoy.

faq.q: Can I bring my own food and drinks?
faq.a: Yes, you are welcome to bring your own food and additional drinks on board. Our stocked bar includes complimentary beer, wine, cava, soft drinks, and water. If you want a catered meal, let us know in advance and we can arrange it.

faq.q: How do I book a yacht for the flamenco festival?
faq.a: Contact us via WhatsApp for real-time availability and pricing. We recommend booking at least two weeks before the festival. For Tier A boats, you can book directly online—other boats require a personalised quote.

Cancellations made more than 14 days before the charter receive a full refund. Between 7 and 14 days, a 50% refund applies. Less than 7 days, no refund. We recommend travel insurance for peace of mind.
There are no age restrictions for passengers. Children are welcome on all boats. Life jackets are available in child sizes. The skipper will brief everyone on safety before departure.

data.body_html:
Plan your marbella flamenco festival yacht charter for 2026 and watch the fiery rhythms of flamenco from the best seat in the house—your private boat. Departing from Puerto Banús, our fleet of 18 boats offers front-row sea views of performances along the Marbella coastline. Whether you want a quick two-hour escape or an all-day luxury cruise, we have a boat to match your group size and budget.

Why charter a boat for the Flamenco Festival?

Watching flamenco from a boat is a game-changer. You avoid the packed terraces, the queues for drinks, and the struggle for a good view. Instead, you anchor just offshore, with the sound of guitar and palmas carrying across the water. The cool sea breeze, a glass of cava in hand, and the sunset behind La Concha—that's the Marbella flamenco experience.

Our charters include a licensed skipper, fuel, drinks, snacks, and insurance. You just show up at Puerto Banús and step aboard. Prices start at €749/2h for a Tier A boat like the Astondoa 40 or Azimut 39.

Best boats for the festival

We have boats for every group size. Here are our top recommendations:

  • Tier A (€749/2h – €2,299/8h): Astondoa 40 (9 pax) or Azimut 39 (11 pax). Ideal for couples or small groups. Includes skipper, drinks, snacks.
  • Tier B (€4,719/4h minimum): Mangusta 80 (12 pax) in white or grey. Includes a free jet ski. Perfect for a party crowd.
  • Other boats: For larger groups, consider the Ferretti 94 (29m, 12 pax) or the Maiora 26m (26m, 12 pax). Pricing on request via WhatsApp.

All boats depart from Puerto Banús, the heart of Marbella's luxury marina. For a full list, see our boat rental Marbella page.

What to expect on the water

Your charter includes a stocked bar with water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, and cava. Light snacks are provided. You can bring your own food if you like—many guests order tapas from a local caterer and have it delivered to the marina.

The skipper will take you to the best spots for viewing the festival. Depending on the day's schedule, you might anchor near Cala del Faro or Cala Cortés for a clear view of the stage. If you want to swim between shows, we can stop at Cabopino or Estepona.

Sample itinerary for festival day

TimeActivity
17:00Meet at Puerto Banús. Board your boat. Safety briefing.
17:30Depart marina. Cruise along the Golden Mile towards Marbella.
18:00Anchor near the festival venue. Enjoy drinks and snacks.
19:00Flamenco performance begins. Watch from the deck.
20:30Sunset cruise. Swim stop at Cala del Faro.
21:30Return to Puerto Banús. Disembark.

This is just a sample. You can customise the timing and route. Contact us via WhatsApp to tailor your day.

How to book

Booking is simple. For Tier A boats, you can reserve directly online. For all other boats, message us on WhatsApp with your preferred date, group size, and boat type. We'll confirm availability and send a personalised quote within hours.

We recommend booking at least two weeks before the festival. Boats fill up quickly, especially the Mangusta 80 and the Azimut 58 Flybridge. For more tips, read our full festival guide.

Nearby attractions

Puerto Banús is the ideal base. Before or after your charter, explore the Golden Mile with its designer boutiques and beach clubs. For a quieter evening, head to Cabopino for fresh seafood. If you want to extend your trip, consider chartering for a day and cruising to Sotogrande or Estepona.

The festival itself often features performances at Río Verde and the Marbella Marina. Your skipper will know the exact locations and can position the boat for the best view.

Frequently asked questions

When is the Marbella Flamenco Festival 2026?

The Marbella Flamenco Festival typically takes place in July, with exact dates announced a few months prior. Check the official Marbella tourism website for 2026 dates. We recommend booking your yacht charter early, as boats fill up fast during festival week.

Can I watch flamenco shows from a boat?

Yes. Some festival performances take place along the Marbella coastline or at venues near the water, such as the Puerto Banús marina. A private boat lets you anchor close to the action, giving you a unique sea-level perspective without the crowds on land.

Which boat is best for a group of 10?

For a group of 10, the Azimut 39 (11 pax) or the Fairline Targa 12m (10 pax) are excellent choices. Both depart from Puerto Banús and include a skipper, drinks, and snacks. Pricing for these boats starts at €749/2h.

What is included in the charter price?

Every charter includes a licensed skipper, fuel, water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava, light snacks, insurance, safety equipment, and Spanish IVA (21%). There are no hidden fees—just show up and enjoy.

Can I bring my own food and drinks?

Yes, you are welcome to bring your own food and additional drinks on board. Our stocked bar includes complimentary beer, wine, cava, soft drinks, and water. If you want a catered meal, let us know in advance and we can arrange it.

How do I book a yacht for the flamenco festival?

Contact us via WhatsApp for real-time availability and pricing. We recommend booking at least two weeks before the festival. For Tier A boats, you can book directly online—other boats require a personalised quote.

What is the cancellation policy?

Cancellations made more than 14 days before the charter receive a full refund. Between 7 and 14 days, a 50% refund applies. Less than 7 days, no refund. We recommend travel insurance for peace of mind.

Are there any age restrictions?

There are no age restrictions for passengers. Children are welcome on all boats. Life jackets are available in child sizes. The skipper will brief everyone on safety before departure.
